question.text: In the depository settlement process, 'pay-out' refers to:

answer.text: The CC delivering securities to buying brokers and funds to selling brokers
The broker paying commission to the exchange
The investor paying margin to the CC
The DP paying fees to SEBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Pay-out is when the CC delivers: securities to buying brokers' CM accounts, and funds to selling brokers. **The Trap:** 'Investor paying margin' is a pre-trade requirement, not part of the pay-out process. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII covers the complete settlement cycle. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Pay-in and pay-out happen at designated times. Pay-out typically follows pay-in once the CC has netted all obligations and verified completeness.
